title: Crucifera juncea
intro:

If you would like, I can write a paragraph on Rorippa juncea instead:

The bioactive constituents of Rorippa juncea include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. taxonomy: Crucifera juncea is a member of the Brassicaceae family, also known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as crucifers. The genus Crucifera does not specifically exist, and it's likely referring to Brassica juncea, which is a species within the Brassicaceae family. Brassica juncea is further classified into several subspecies, including B. j. juncea, B. j. integrifolia, and B. j. multibracteata. The species is native to the Indian subcontinent and has been naturalized in many parts of the world.
